differences.title: 1. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Easier to pick up, especially for beginners due to its progressive nature and clear documentation.
differences.rightDesc: More complex due to its opinionated structure, RxJS, and TypeScript, requiring more upfront learning.

differences.title: 2. ARCHITECTURE
differences.leftDesc: Can be adopted incrementally, integrating into existing projects or used for simple SPAs.
differences.rightDesc: Full-fledged framework with a defined structure, best suited for large, complex enterprise applications.

differences.title: 3. TEMPLATING
differences.leftDesc: Uses HTML templates with directives for a familiar and intuitive development experience.
differences.rightDesc: Uses HTML templates augmented with decorators and specific syntax for binding and directives.

differences.title: 4. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Built-in solutions like Vuex or Pinia, simple to integrate for managing global state.
differences.rightDesc: Relies on RxJS-based NgRx or service injection for more complex state management patterns.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Generally smaller bundle sizes and faster initial load times due to its progressive nature.
differences.rightDesc: Can have larger bundle sizes but optimized for complex applications with ahead-of-time compilation.

differences.title: 6. COMMUNITY & ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Large and active community, extensive libraries and tools, excellent documentation.
differences.rightDesc: Strong enterprise adoption, comprehensive tooling, and a well-established, albeit sometimes slower-moving, ecosystem.

differences.title: 7. TOOLING
differences.leftDesc: Offers flexibility with options like Vue CLI, Vite, and various IDE integrations.
differences.rightDesc: Provides a powerful, integrated CLI (Angular CLI) for scaffolding, building, and testing.
